VMCalendar.java

00019  ***************************************************************************/
00020 
00021 package jelatine;
00022 
00023 import java.util.Calendar;
00024 import java.util.Date;
00025 
00029 public final class VMCalendar extends Calendar
00030 {
00034     public static final int BC = 0;
00035 
00039     public static final int AD = 1;
00040 
00050     private long gregorianCutover = (new Date((24 * 60 * 60 * 1000L)
00051         * (((1582 * (365 * 4 + 1)) / 4 + (java.util.Calendar.OCTOBER
00052         * (31 + 30 + 31 + 30 + 31) - 9) / 5 + 5) - ((1970 * (365 * 4 + 1))
00053         / 4 + 1 - 13)))).getTime();
00054 
00070     private static final int EPOCH_DAYS = 719162;
00071 
00076     public VMCalendar()
00077     {
00078         super();
00079         setTimeInMillis(System.currentTimeMillis());
00080         complete();
00081     }
00082 
00086     protected void computeTime()
00087     {
00088         int millisInDay = 0;
00089         int year = fields[YEAR];
00090         int month = fields[MONTH];
00091         int day = fields[DAY_OF_MONTH];
00092 
00093         int minute = fields[MINUTE];
00094         int second = fields[SECOND];
00095         int millis = fields[MILLISECOND];
00096         int[] month_days = { 31, 28, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31 };
00097         int[] dayCount = { 0, 31, 59, 90, 120, 151, 181, 212, 243, 273, 304, 334 };
00098         int hour = 0;
00099 
00100         // rest of code assumes day/month/year set
00101         // should negative BC years be AD?
00102         // get the hour (but no check for validity)
00103         if (isSet[HOUR])
00104         {
00105             hour = fields[HOUR];
00106 
00107             if (fields[AM_PM] == PM)
00108                 hour += 12;
00109         }
00110         else
00111             hour = fields[HOUR_OF_DAY];
00112 
00113         // Read the era,year,month,day fields and convert as appropriate.
00114         // Calculate number of milliseconds into the day
00115         // This takes care of both h, m, s, ms over/underflows.
00116         long allMillis = (((hour * 60L) + minute) * 60L + second) * 1000L + millis;
00117         day += allMillis / (24 * 60 * 60 * 1000L);
00118         millisInDay = (int) (allMillis % (24 * 60 * 60 * 1000L));
00119 
00120         if (month < 0)
00121         {
00122             year += (int) month / 12;
00123             month = month % 12;
00124             if (month < 0)
00125             {
00126                 month += 12;
00127                 year--;
00128             }
00129         }
00130         if (month > 11)
00131         {
00132             year += (month / 12);
00133             month = month % 12;
00134         }
00135 
00136         month_days[1] = isLeapYear(year) ? 29 : 28;
00137 
00138         while (day <= 0)
00139         {
00140             if (month == 0)
00141             {
00142                 year--;
00143                 month_days[1] = isLeapYear(year) ? 29 : 28;
00144             }
00145 
00146             month = (month + 11) % 12;
00147             day += month_days[month];
00148         }
00149         while (day > month_days[month])
00150         {
00151             day -= (month_days[month]);
00152             month = (month + 1) % 12;
00153 
00154             if (month == 0)
00155             {
00156                 year++;
00157                 month_days[1] = isLeapYear(year) ? 29 : 28;
00158             }
00159         }
00160 
00161         // ok, by here we have valid day,month,year,era and millisinday
00162         int dayOfYear = dayCount[month] + day - 1; // (day starts on 1)
00163         if (isLeapYear(year) && month > 1)
00164             dayOfYear++;
00165 
00166         int relativeDay = (year - 1) * 365 + ((year - 1) >> 2) + dayOfYear
00167             - EPOCH_DAYS; // gregorian days from 1 to epoch.
00168         int gregFactor = (int) Math.floor((double) (year - 1) / 400.)
00169             - (int) Math.floor((double) (year - 1) / 100.);
00170 
00171         if ((relativeDay + gregFactor) * 60L * 60L * 24L * 1000L >= gregorianCutover)
00172             relativeDay += gregFactor;
00173         else
00174             relativeDay -= 2;
00175 
00176         time = relativeDay * (24 * 60 * 60 * 1000L) + millisInDay;
00177 
00178         // the epoch was a Thursday.
00179         int weekday = (int) (relativeDay + THURSDAY) % 7;
00180 
00181         if (weekday <= 0)
00182             weekday += 7;
00183 
00184         fields[DAY_OF_WEEK] = weekday;
00185 
00186         /* No Time zone corrections are required since the only time zone
00187          * which must be supported by the CLDC spec is GMT. */
00188 
00189         isTimeSet = true;
00190     }
00191 
00195     protected void computeFields()
00196     {
00197         boolean gregorian = (time >= gregorianCutover);
00198 
00199 
00200         long day = time / (24 * 60 * 60 * 1000L);
00201         int millisInDay = (int) (time % (24 * 60 * 60 * 1000L));
00202 
00203         if (millisInDay < 0)
00204         {
00205             millisInDay += (24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
00206             day--;
00207         }
00208 
00209         calculateDay(fields, day, gregorian);
00210 
00211         if (millisInDay >= 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000)
00212         {
00213             millisInDay -= 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
00214             calculateDay(fields, ++day, gregorian);
00215         }
00216 
00217         int hourOfDay = millisInDay / (60 * 60 * 1000);
00218         fields[AM_PM] = (hourOfDay < 12) ? AM : PM;
00219         int hour = hourOfDay % 12;
00220         fields[HOUR] = hour;
00221         fields[HOUR_OF_DAY] = hourOfDay;
00222         millisInDay %= (60 * 60 * 1000);
00223         fields[MINUTE] = millisInDay / (60 * 1000);
00224         millisInDay %= (60 * 1000);
00225         fields[SECOND] = millisInDay / (1000);
00226         fields[MILLISECOND] = millisInDay % 1000;
00227 
00228         areFieldsSet = isSet[YEAR] = isSet[MONTH] = isSet[DAY_OF_MONTH]
00229             = isSet[DAY_OF_WEEK] = isSet[AM_PM] = isSet[HOUR]
00230             = isSet[HOUR_OF_DAY] = isSet[MINUTE] = isSet[SECOND]
00231             = isSet[MILLISECOND] = true;
00232     }
00233 
00234     private boolean isLeapYear(int year)
00235     {
00236         // Only years divisible by 4 can be leap years
00237         if ((year & 3) != 0)
00238             return false;
00239 
00240         // Is the leap-day a Julian date? Then it's a leap year
00241         if (! isGregorian(year, 31 + 29 - 1))
00242             return true;
00243 
00244         // Apply gregorian rules otherwise
00245         return ((year % 100) != 0 || (year % 400) == 0);
00246     }
00247 
00248     private boolean isGregorian(int year, int dayOfYear)
00249     {
00250         int relativeDay = (year - 1) * 365 + ((year - 1) >> 2) + dayOfYear
00251             - EPOCH_DAYS; // gregorian days from 1 to epoch.
00252         int gregFactor = (int) Math.floor((double) (year - 1) / 400.)
00253             - (int) Math.floor((double) (year - 1) / 100.);
00254 
00255         return ((relativeDay + gregFactor) * 60L * 60L * 24L * 1000L >= gregorianCutover);
00256     }
00257 
00266     private void calculateDay(int[] fields, long day, boolean gregorian)
00267     {
00268         // the epoch was a Thursday.
00269         int weekday = (int) (day + THURSDAY) % 7;
00270 
00271         if (weekday <= 0)
00272             weekday += 7;
00273 
00274         fields[DAY_OF_WEEK] = weekday;
00275 
00276         // get a first approximation of the year.  This may be one
00277         // year too big.
00278         int year = 1970 + (int) (gregorian
00279             ? ((day - 100L) * 400L) / (365L * 400L + 100L - 4L
00280             + 1L) : ((day - 100L) * 4L) / (365L * 4L + 1L));
00281 
00282         if (day >= 0)
00283             year++;
00284 
00285         long firstDayOfYear = getLinearDay(year, 1, gregorian);
00286 
00287         // Now look in which year day really lies.
00288         if (day < firstDayOfYear)
00289         {
00290             year--;
00291             firstDayOfYear = getLinearDay(year, 1, gregorian);
00292         }
00293 
00294         day -= firstDayOfYear - 1; // day of year,  one based.
00295 
00296         if (year <= 0)
00297         {
00298             fields[YEAR] = 1 - year;
00299         }
00300         else
00301         {
00302             fields[YEAR] = year;
00303         }
00304 
00305         int leapday = isLeapYear(year) ? 1 : 0;
00306 
00307         if (day <= 31 + 28 + leapday)
00308         {
00309             fields[MONTH] = (int) day / 32; // 31->JANUARY, 32->FEBRUARY
00310             fields[DAY_OF_MONTH] = (int) day - 31 * fields[MONTH];
00311         }
00312         else
00313         {
00314             // A few more magic formulas
00315             int scaledDay = ((int) day - leapday) * 5 + 8;
00316             fields[MONTH] = scaledDay / (31 + 30 + 31 + 30 + 31);
00317             fields[DAY_OF_MONTH] = (scaledDay % (31 + 30 + 31 + 30 + 31)) / 5 + 1;
00318         }
00319     }
00320 
00332     private long getLinearDay(int year, int dayOfYear, boolean gregorian)
00333     {
00334         // The 13 is the number of days, that were omitted in the Gregorian
00335         // Calender until the epoch.
00336         // We shift right by 2 instead of dividing by 4, to get correct
00337         // results for negative years (and this is even more efficient).
00338         long julianDay = (year - 1) * 365L + ((year - 1) >> 2) + (dayOfYear - 1)
00339             - EPOCH_DAYS; // gregorian days from 1 to epoch.
00340 
00341         if (gregorian)
00342         {
00343             // subtract the days that are missing in gregorian calendar
00344             // with respect to julian calendar.
00345             //
00346             // Okay, here we rely on the fact that the gregorian
00347             // calendar was introduced in the AD era.  This doesn't work
00348             // with negative years.
00349             //
00350             // The additional leap year factor accounts for the fact that
00351             // a leap day is not seen on Jan 1 of the leap year.
00352             int gregOffset = (int) Math.floor((double) (year - 1) / 400.)
00353                 - (int) Math.floor((double) (year - 1) / 100.);
00354 
00355             return julianDay + gregOffset;
00356         }
00357         else
00358             julianDay -= 2;
00359         return julianDay;
00360     }
00361 
00362 }
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:25 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
